title: Present Simple – Negative
description: How to form negative sentences in the Present Simple

lessons.title: Negative Structure
lessons.content:
**Structure:** Subject + do/does + not + Verb

For most pronouns: use **do not** (don't)
For **he / she / it** (third-person singular): use **does not** (doesn't) — and do NOT add 's' to the verb!

| Pronoun | Helper | Example |
|---------|--------|---------|
| I | do not | I do not eat apples. |
| You | do not | You do not eat apples. |
| We | do not | We do not eat apples. |
| They | do not | They do not eat apples. |
| He | does not | He does not eat apples. |
| She | does not | She does not eat apples. |
| It | does not | It does not eat apples. |

lessons.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
lessons.content:
⚠️ Do NOT use 'am not / is not / are not' before the verb.
⚠️ Do NOT add '-ing' to the verb.
⚠️ When using 'does not/doesn't', keep the main verb in its BASE form (no 's')!

| ❌ Incorrect | ✅ Correct |
|-------------|----------|
| She is not study French. | She **does not** study French. |
| I am not understand you. | I **do not** understand you. |
| We are not living in Haifa. | We **don't** live in Haifa. |
| He not working out every day. | He **doesn't** work out every day. |

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — use 'don't watch'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Jill/She) — use 'doesn't eat'. The verb stays in the base form.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person plural (Children/They) — use 'don't go'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (My friend/He/She) — use 'doesn't like'. Keep base verb form.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (It) — use 'doesn't snow'. Keep base verb form.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Simon/He) — use 'doesn't cook'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — use 'don't tell'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — use 'don't do'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (The dog/It) — use 'doesn't like'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Jim/He) — use 'doesn't want'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — use 'don't play'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.sentence: I {_} because I am afraid of airplanes.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — use 'don't fly'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person plural (They) — use 'don't speak'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (He) — use 'doesn't write'. Keep base verb form.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — use 'don't have'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Ice/It) — use 'doesn't melt'. Keep base verb form.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.sentence: I normally {_} singing in the morning.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — use 'don't practice'.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence in the negative form using the verb in brackets.
exercises.sentence: I {_} that is a good idea.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — use 'don't think'. 'Think' is a stative verb.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — add 'don't' before the verb.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
answers: She does not live in Jerusalem.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (She) — use 'doesn't' and keep the base verb form (live, not lives).

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
answers: I do not like fried eggs.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — add 'don't' before the verb.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
answers: We don't study together every week.
We do not study together every week.
exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — add 'don't' before the verb.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
answers: The dog doesn't want the bone.
The dog does not want the bone.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (The dog/It) — use 'doesn't want'. Notice the verb loses its 's'.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
exercises.explanation: Second person (You) — add 'don't' before the verb.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
exercises.explanation: Third person plural (They) — add 'don't' before the verb.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
exercises.sentence: I eat with my family once a week.
answers: I don't eat with my family once a week.
I do not eat with my family once a week.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — add 'don't' before the verb.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
answers: Andy does not make good food.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Andy/He) — use 'doesn't make'. Notice the verb loses its 's'.

exercises.instruction: Change the sentence to the negative form.
exercises.sentence: I read the newspaper every morning.
answers: I don't read the newspaper every morning.
I do not read the newspaper every morning.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — add 'don't' before the verb.
